payload.summary: Make the Next.js/Fumadocs site build as a GitHub Pages-compatible static export without regressing the existing docs shell, routes, or customer-visible Phase 1 behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: next.config.ts and any required app/router config support GitHub Pages static export.
The exported site resolves assets and routes correctly for GitHub Pages hosting, including docs and tags routes.
Build-time commands document and verify the export artifact shape instead of assuming only next start.
Phase 1 route/search/static validations stay green or are updated in a targeted way with deterministic tests.
validation: export-specific build verification for the generated static artifact

payload.summary: Finish the static search path so GitHub Pages can serve the current Phase 1 search experience without relying on a live Node runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Search uses a GitHub Pages-compatible static data source in production/export mode.
Existing GQA, attention, and KV cache Phase 1 search expectations still pass against the static site path.
The implementation reuses the current search/export machinery where possible instead of introducing a second search system.
Tests cover the static handoff contract.
validation: Phase 1 search verification against the static/exported site
payload.coverageExpectation: Maintain at least 90% reachable coverage for any touched reusable search component, or document thin-wrapper smoke coverage if applicable.

payload.summary: Wire GitHub Actions deployment for GitHub Pages and align README/operations/planner docs so Phase 1 describes the live publish path instead of deferment language.
payload.implementationStatus: Complete in repository: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` publishes `out/` on `main` pushes using `make build-export` with `GITHUB_PAGES_BASE_PATH: ai-model-reference`; `docs/operations.md` and `README.md` document active deployment, permissions, deploy-check visibility, and commit-SHA traceability.
acceptanceCriteria: A GitHub Pages deploy workflow exists and is consistent with the repository's static export path.
Operations/readme/planner docs describe the real deploy behavior instead of deferment language.
Deployment status and required permissions are documented concretely enough for maintainers to use.
Planner guidance documents `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` as the active publish workflow.
validation: workflow file lint by repository tests/build where available
documentation/tests updated for deployment guidance drift

payload.summary: Add a narrow validator pass that proves the GitHub Pages/export path works and that the active Phase 1 customer ask can actually stop after this repair.
acceptanceCriteria: The validator exercises the built/exported artifact rather than only next start.
It reports pass/fail/uncertain evidence for GitHub Pages-specific rows plus the existing core Phase 1 routes/search expectations that could regress.
Planner-facing output is concrete enough to choose between one more narrow repair and true Phase 1 stop-and-wait.
validation: documented command path for planner use

payload.summary: Re-enter ideafy after the GitHub Pages repair batch completes so the planner can run convergence synthesis on the exported artifact and decide whether Phase 1 can stop. Deploy automation under `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` is already wired; remaining closure is validator evidence plus customer phase update.
